name: Newton's Laws of Motion: The Framework of Classical Mechanics
chatResponse: I will guide you through the logical structure of Newton's Laws, illustrating how they govern the relationship between forces and the motion of objects.

steps.title: The First Law: Equilibrium and Inertia
steps.content:
Newton's First Law (the Law of Inertia) defines the 'default' state of an object. If the sum of all external forces acting on a body is zero ($$\sum F = 0$$), the object maintains its state of motion. 

- **At Rest:** Velocity stays zero.
- **In Motion:** Velocity stays constant (straight line, constant speed).
- **Inertia:** The resistance of any physical object to any change in its velocity.

steps.title: The Second Law: The Fundamental Equation
steps.content:
When a net force is applied, the object's velocity changes, resulting in acceleration. This law provides the mathematical link between force, mass, and acceleration:

$$F = m \cdot a$$

Where:
- **F** is the Net Force (Newtons)
- **m** is Mass (Kilograms)
- **a** is Acceleration ($$m/s^2$$)

This means acceleration is directly proportional to force and inversely proportional to mass.

steps.title: The Third Law: Symmetry in Interaction
steps.content:
Forces are not things an object *has*, but rather *interactions* between two objects. For every action force exerted by object A on object B, there is an equal and opposite reaction force exerted by object B on object A:

$$F_{A \rightarrow B} = -F_{B \rightarrow A}$$

Crucially, these two forces act on **different** objects, which is why they do not cancel each other out in a single-body diagram.

visualCode:
graph TD
    Start[Newton's Laws] --> L1[1st Law: Inertia]
    Start --> L2[2nd Law: Acceleration]
    Start --> L3[3rd Law: Action/Reaction]

    L1 --> L1_Cond{Net Force = 0?}
    L1_Cond -->|Yes| L1_Res[Constant Velocity / Rest]

    L2 --> L2_Math[F = m * a]
    L2_Math --> L2_Rel[Force ↑ : Accel ↑]
    L2_Math --> L2_Mass[Mass ↑ : Accel ↓]

    L3 --> L3_Pair[Force Pairs]
    L3_Pair --> L3_Eq[Equal Magnitude]
    L3_Pair --> L3_Opp[Opposite Direction]

    style Start fill:#1e293b,stroke:#0ea5e9,color:#fff
    style L1 fill:#0f172a,stroke:#ec4899,color:#fff
    style L2 fill:#0f172a,stroke:#10b981,color:#fff
    style L3 fill:#0f172a,stroke:#f59e0b,color:#fff
    style Default fill:none,stroke:#64748b,stroke-width:2px,stroke-dasharray: 5 5
